com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXConnector

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXAbstractConnector, IXAbstractPinList, IXConnectivityObject, IXConnectorBaseObject, IXInternalPositionedObject, IXInternalPositionsContainer, IXLibrariedObject, IXModuledObject, IXObject, IXPinList<IXAbstractPin>, IXWriteableObject
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXRefinedConnector

    public interface IXConnector
extends IXConnectorBaseObject, IXInternalPositionsContainer, IXInternalPositionedObject
    Interface for accessing backshells, mated device connectors, owning harness, bundles connected to and nodes of a harness-side connector.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Deprecated Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String geAncestorModularConnectorNames()
      Deprecated. 
      The procedure for getting modular connector is changed, instead use getOccupiedPosition() and getPositionContainer().

      Get the backshell owned by this connector.

      Get the connector refinement information for this IXConnector

      Get the associated physical connectors of a connector within a Capital Topology design.

      Get the primary position connector if the connector is a variant position connector.
      java.util.Set<IXConnector> getVariantPositionConnectors()
      Get the variant position connectors if the connector have variant position connectors.
      boolean isInline()
      Flag to indicate whether this connector is an inline connector.

    • Method Detail

      • getBackshell

        IXBackshell getBackshell()
        Get the backshell owned by this connector.
        Returns:
        the subordinate backshell if one exists, null otherwise.

      • getVariantPositionConnectors

        java.util.Set<IXConnector> getVariantPositionConnectors()
        Get the variant position connectors if the connector have variant position connectors.
        Returns:
        the variant position connectors if exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getPrimaryPositionConnector

        IXConnector getPrimaryPositionConnector()
        Get the primary position connector if the connector is a variant position connector.
        Returns:
        the primary position connector if the connector is a variant position connector, null otherwise.

      • isInline

        boolean isInline()
        Flag to indicate whether this connector is an inline connector.
        Returns:
        boolean True if this connector is an inline connector.

      • getPhysicalConnectors

        java.util.Set<? extends IXConnector> getPhysicalConnectors()
        Get the associated physical connectors of a connector within a Capital Topology design. For connectors within other design types this will return an empty set.

        Physical connectors are associated to connectors within a Topology Design during the Wiring Sync process (on design load). This association is controlled either by constraints placed upon the Topology design or by matching connector names to guide the association.

        If the connector has no associated physical connectors this method will return an empty set.

        The physical connectors returned by this method can be used to determine pin mappings.
        If the connector has a single associated physical connector this will be the only entry in the returned set and pin mappings may be inferred by common pin naming.

        Alternatively, the returned set may contain multiple physical connector instances. In this case they may be either:
        • instances of a common shared object. In this case the pin mappings will also be inferred by name.
        • instances that do not reference a common shared object. In this case the connector refinement information must be queried to obtain pin mapping information. This is typically the case for physical connectors that have either modular or m:n connector references.
        Returns:
        A set of physical connectors from the source wiring designs that have been associated with this connector in the Topology plane
        See Also:
        getConnectorRefinement()

      • getConnectorRefinement

        IXConnectorRefinement getConnectorRefinement()
        Get the connector refinement information for this IXConnector

        Within a Capital Integrator or Capital Topology design this method will return a description of the refined connectors that have been specified for this IXConnector or null if no refinement has been specified. Within other design types this will return null.

        Within Capital Topology, this refinement information is used to define the structure of the physical connectors to which this abstract connector refers in the physical designs.
        For example, where a modular connector is specified in the wiring design, the connector in the topology design may represent the modular housing, and the refinement may defines the children within the modular hierarchy. The refinement information will contain a 1:1 mapping to the connectors in the wiring design (where the connector in the topology design may instead claim to have a 1:N mapping to them through the getPhysicalConnectors() method).

        Returns:
        An IXConnectorRefinement object from which refined details can be obtained. Returns null if the connector has no refinement.
        See Also:
        getPhysicalConnectors()

      • geAncestorModularConnectorNames

        java.lang.String geAncestorModularConnectorNames()
        Deprecated. The procedure for getting modular connector is changed, instead use getOccupiedPosition() and getPositionContainer().
        Get the parent modularconnector name
        Returns:
        Concatenated names of all the ancestor modular connectors if present, otherwise empty string
